taxonomy: Hieracium subandurense belongs to the family Asteraceae and is a species of hawkweed, specifically classified within the genus Hieracium. It is a type of flowering plant, native to the Andes mountain range in South America. Further classification places Hieracium subandurense as a member of the subgenus Hieracium subgenus Pilosella and the tribe Senecioneae. The species is characterized by its small yellow flowers and grows in rocky and alpine environments. This plant's specific classification is essential for understanding its evolutionary relationships and ecological adaptations.
